title: Positive Imperfect Tense
description: Master the imperfect tense (imperfekti) for talking about completed past actions - the storytelling tense!

questions.text: What is the basic formula for forming the imperfect tense?
questions.explanation: The imperfect is formed by inserting the marker 'i' between the verb stem and the personal ending. Example: nuku + i + n = nukuin (I slept).

questions.text: What is special about the 3rd person singular (hän) in the imperfect?
options: It uses the same ending as 1st person
It has NO personal ending - just stem + 'i'
It uses a double personal ending
questions.explanation: In imperfect, 3rd person singular has no ending: hän nuk-ui (she slept), not nukuin. This is different from present tense (nukkuu).

questions.text: Which vowels DROP when you add the 'i' marker in imperfect?
options: Only back vowels (o, u, ö, y)
Only front vowels (a, ä, e, i)
questions.explanation: Front vowels (a, ä, e, i) disappear when you insert 'i'. Back vowels (o, u, ö, y) stay. Example: odotan → odotin (the 'a' drops).

questions.text: Form the imperfect 1st person for: nukkua (to sleep). Show your work.
questions.explanation: nukkua → stem is 'nuku' (no vowel drop for back vowel 'u') + i + n = nukuin. The double 'kk' stays because this is type 1 verb in present form.

questions.text: Form the imperfect 1st person for: odotan (to wait). Show your work.
correctAnswers: odota + drop a + i + n = odotin
questions.explanation: odotan → stem is 'odota' but 'a' drops → odo + i + n = odotin. The front vowel 'a' is removed when inserting 'i'.

questions.text: What happens to diphthongs (two vowels together) in imperfect?
options: Only the first vowel of the diphthong drops, then 'i' is added
questions.explanation: Drop the FIRST vowel of the diphthong. Example: vie (take) → drop 'i' → ve + i + n = vein. Another: syö → drop 'y' → sö + i + n = söin.

questions.text: Form the imperfect 1st person for: juoda (to drink). Show your work.
correctAnswers: juo + drop u + i + n = join
questions.explanation: juoda → stem 'juo' (diphthong) → drop 'u' → jo + i + n = join. The first vowel of 'uo' drops when you insert 'i'.

questions.text: What marker does Verb Type 4 use instead of 'i' in the imperfect?
questions.explanation: Type 4 verbs (like haluta, tietää) use '-si-' instead of '-i-' in imperfect. Example: haluan → halusin (I wanted), not haluiin.

questions.text: Conjugate 'haluta' (to want) in the imperfect for mä (1st person). Identify the verb type.
questions.explanation: haluta is Type 4, so use '-si-' marker: halua + si + n = halusin. NOT 'haluiin' (which would use the Type 1 pattern).

questions.text: Which sentence correctly uses the imperfect to describe a completed action?
questions.explanation: The imperfect (olin) is correct here because you're talking about a specific past time (viikonloppuna = over the weekend). The action is completed.

questions.text: Write 3 sentences about what you did last weekend using the imperfect tense. Use verbs: mennä, syödä, katsoa.
questions.explanation: Examples: 'Mä menin kauppaan.' 'Mä söin pizzaa.' 'Mä katsoin elokuvan.' All use imperfect for past completed actions with time context.

questions.text: Which of these has the WRONG imperfect form?
options: Hän luki kirjan (She read a book)
Me mentiin ulos (We went out - spoken form)
Mä puhuin Liisalle (I spoke to Lisa)
questions.explanation: 'Hän olet' is wrong—it mixes person forms. Correct: 'Hän oli kotona' (she was home). The others are all correct imperfect forms.

questions.text: What is special about irregular verbs like 'olla' in the imperfect? Provide the full conjugation for 'olla'.
questions.explanation: Irregular verbs don't follow the standard pattern. 'Olla': mä olin, sä olit, hän oli, me oltiin, te olitte, ne oli. These must be memorized!

questions.text: When should you use imperfect tense?
options: For actions that were completed in a specific past time
For actions that might happen in the future
For actions that are still happening
questions.explanation: The imperfect describes finished, completed past actions. Usually there's a time marker (eilen = yesterday, viime kesänä = last summer) indicating when it happened.
